title: erycibe subsericea
intro: Erycibe subsericea, also known as the hairy erycibe or hairy moonseed, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating various skin conditions. The plant is prepared in various herbal forms, including decoctions, infusions, and tonics, which are traditionally used to treat various ailments in folk medicine.
